questions.question: Which of the following is a key assumption of the Naive Bayes classifier?
options: C) The classes are mutually exclusive
questions.explanation: The Naive Bayes classifier assumes that each feature is independent of the others given the class label.

questions.question: In a Naive Bayes classifier for sentiment analysis, which feature is typically used as the most important feature?
options: A) The word frequency of positive words
B) The word frequency of negative words
C) The length of the sentence
D) The average sentiment score of the text
questions.explanation: In sentiment analysis, the word frequency of positive words is typically used as the most important feature.

questions.question: What is a common optimization technique for Naive Bayes classifiers in text classification tasks?
options: B) Using a more complex feature extraction technique
C) Using a smoothing technique to reduce overfitting
D) Using a different classifier altogether
questions.explanation: A common optimization technique for Naive Bayes classifiers is to use a smoothing technique to reduce overfitting.

questions.question: In which of the following tasks can Naive Bayes classifiers be used as a language model?
questions.explanation: Naive Bayes classifiers can be used as a language model in all of the above tasks.

questions.question: What is a common assumption made by Naive Bayes classifiers in text classification tasks?
options: A) The classes are mutually exclusive
questions.explanation: Naive Bayes classifiers assume that the features are independent of each other given the class label.
